function.name: memorise
function.description: Stores a fact or piece of information you want to remember during conversations and or action output you want to remember for further action taking.

fact.description: The fact or information you want to remember.

function.name: reconsolidation
function.description: Updates an existing memorised fact in place. Use this when a fact changes (e.g. user's age, job, location, preferences) instead of adding a conflicting entry. Finds the fact by exact match on the current text and replaces it.

current_fact.description: The exact text of the existing fact to update.

updated_fact.description: The new text to replace it with.

function.name: search_skills
function.description: Searches all available skill schemas for tools matching a given tag and returns their names and descriptions. Call this FIRST before load_skills to discover what tools are available.

function.name: load_skills
function.description: Loads full tool schemas by name so they become callable. Resets previously loaded tools. Must be preceded by search_skills — only the names returned by search_skills can be loaded. Load ALL tools for a task in a single call.

function.name: workspace_list
function.description: Lists macOS desktops (Spaces) and windows within them. Calls list_desktops to see all Spaces, list_desktop_windows with a specific index to see its windows, or list_all_windows to see windows on every Space grouped by desktop.

type.description: 'desktops' — list all desktops by number. 'desktop_windows' — list windows on a specific desktop (requires desktop_index). 'all_windows' — list windows across all desktops grouped by desktop.

desktop_index.description: The desktop (Space) number to query. Required when type is 'desktop_windows'. Numbers start at 1.

function.name: clipboard_read
function.description: Reads the current content from the macOS system clipboard (pasteboard) using pbpaste. Returns text content. If the clipboard contains non-text data (images, files), reports the byte size. Returns an explicit message if the clipboard is empty.

function.name: clipboard_write
function.description: Writes text to the macOS system clipboard (pasteboard) using pbcopy. Overwrites whatever is currently on the clipboard.

text.description: The text content to copy to the clipboard.

function.name: grep
function.description: Searches file contents for a regex pattern and returns matching lines with file paths and line numbers. Tries ripgrep (rg) first — much faster for large codebases — and falls back to Python's re module if rg is not installed.

pattern.description: Regex pattern to search for (e.g. 'def \w+', 'TODO', 'error.*timeout'). Supports all Python re syntax.

root.description: Root directory to search from (defaults to home_dir in config.json). Must be inside home_dir.

glob.description: File glob pattern to filter which files are searched (e.g. '**/*.py', '**/*.md', '**/*.{js,ts}'). Defaults to common text-file extensions only.

max_matches.description: Maximum number of matching lines to return (default 50). Prevents overwhelming the context window.

context_lines.description: Number of surrounding lines to include before and after each match for context (default 0).

case_sensitive.description: Whether to match case sensitively (default true). Set to false for case-insensitive search.

function.name: heartbeats_schedule
function.description: Schedules a future autonomous action ('heartbeat') for Evy. Use this for tasks that need checking on later — e.g. checking email responses after sending a message, monitoring for updates, following up on something at a specific time. When the time comes, the heartbeat prompt will be sent to the model autonomously, shown in the chat as a heartbeat response. To make the heartbeat repeat, set the 'repeat' parameter.

name.description: The prompt to send to Evy when the heartbeat fires. Describes what Evy should do (e.g. 'Check email for replies to the message I sent earlier').

time.description: When to run the heartbeat. Accepts ISO datetime (e.g. '2026-07-01T14:30:00') or relative time (e.g. 'in 5 minutes', 'in 2 hours', 'in 1 day').

repeat.description: Optional. If set, the heartbeat reschedules itself after each successful execution. Accepts 'daily', 'hourly', or 'every X minutes/hours/days' (e.g. 'every 30 minutes', 'every 2 hours', 'every 3 days'). Omit for one-shot.

function.name: heartbeats_cancel
function.description: Cancels a previously scheduled heartbeat by its ID or name. The heartbeat(s) will not run.

id.description: The ID of the heartbeat to cancel (returned by heartbeats_schedule). Takes precedence over name if both are given.

name.description: Optional. If provided (without id), cancels ALL pending heartbeats with this name. Useful for stopping repeating heartbeats.

function.name: heartbeats_list
function.description: Lists scheduled heartbeats, optionally filtered by status and/or time range. Useful to check what heartbeats are pending, what has already run, or what failed.

status.description: Filter by status: 'pending' (not yet run), 'in-operation' (currently running), 'success' (completed successfully), 'fail' (failed). If omitted, all statuses are returned.

from_time.description: Start of time range (ISO datetime). Only heartbeats scheduled at or after this time are returned.

to_time.description: End of time range (ISO datetime). Only heartbeats scheduled at or before this time are returned.

function.name: discord_send
function.description: Sends a message to a Discord channel. Use this to proactively send updates, alerts, or information to Discord without being prompted by a Discord message. You need the channel ID — ask the user for it if you don't know it.

channel_id.description: The Discord channel ID to send to (a numeric snowflake, e.g. '123456789012345678').
